summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Mastercard rearranges leadership
articles.description: The card network promoted a pack of new leaders, including to the chief financial officer post, as the old guard moved into other roles.

articles.title: Fintech Wise probed over AML concerns
articles.description: A Belgian prosecutor is looking into “indications of non-compliance,” particularly from “a lack of proper identification of clients and their activities.”

articles.title: Judge blocks Illinois card fee law
articles.description: The injunction for banks and card networks Monday came after U.S. regulators moved to preempt the statute curbing card swipe fees.

articles.title: Illinois lawmakers again delay card fee law
articles.description: The state’s lawmakers voted to hold off on implementing the law as federal bank regulators sought to preempt the statute that curbs swipe fees.

articles.title: PayPal CEO leans on AI chief
articles.description: The company’s new CEO, Enrique Lores, is counting on a newly appointed executive to lead a cost-cutting campaign using artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Fed’s top payments official to exit
articles.description: Mark Gould, who was the Federal Reserve’s first payments chief, said he will retire this year, but stay on while the central bank seeks his successor.

articles.title: Adyen CFO heads for exit
articles.description: The chief financial officer departure comes as the Dutch payments processor seeks to build up its U.S. presence.

articles.title: Trump should nix high-cost loans
articles.description: “If the president wants to provide people relief from high-interest debt ... he should lead a crackdown against high-cost credit schemes,” writes a consumer advocate lawyer.
